preamble: Prothesis of [o] is a characteristic of certain Greek dialects and historical developments, often arising before specific consonant roots.

examples.note: Greek prothetic vowel o- before initial consonant cluster or liquid. The o- prothesis is characteristic of Greek and is one source of words with unexplained initial vowel.
Same prothetic o- development before initial f-cluster in Greek.
Dialectal prothesis of [o] before initial r- in Kouvouklia Modern Greek.
